original_image_tags: A single page of aged, yellowed, and torn paper. The text is printed in reverse (mirrored), indicating this is the back side of a document with ink bleed-through or a carbon copy. The page is heavily damaged, with ragged edges and missing pieces along the left side. Two hole-punch remnants with reinforcing tape are at the top. Faint, reversed text is visible throughout. At the bottom, clearer reversed text includes stamps and handwritten annotations.

elements.description: The back of a paper document. Faint, reversed typed text is visible through the paper. At the bottom, several stamps are visible: a red rectangular stamp reading 'MAILED SF' and 'NOV 3 1954'; a black ink stamp reading 'NOV 30 1954'; a black ink stamp reading 'FBI'; and a typed label on the right edge with department names. A faded rectangular tape mark is near the top left. The paper has irregular, torn edges, particularly on the left and right sides.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content: a damaged, yellowed paper with reversed text and specific stamps at the bottom, but Description B adds detailed verbatim text for stamps not quoted in Description A, creating a detail addition but no direct contradiction.
